title: Audit Spine event (v2 — §4.5)
description: One line of an append-only, hash-chained per-loop ledger at .orchestrator/audit/<loop_id>.jsonl (plan §4.5). MATCHES the event emitted by engine-kit/audit/audit_log.py make_event(): fields {loop_id, seq, ts, type, payload, prev_hash, hash}, in that body order. HASH CHAIN: hash = sha256( prev_hash + canonical_json(event_without_hash) ).hexdigest(), where canonical_json = json.dumps(obj, sort_keys=True, separators=(',', ':')); the first event's prev_hash is 64 zeros (GENESIS_PREV_HASH); seq starts at 0 and increases by 1. payload is a generic dict (the ledger stores whatever it is given); a per-spawn event carries the execution-context payload in $defs/spawn_payload (G3). This schema validates event STRUCTURE; chain integrity is verified by audit_log.verify_chain. Audit is runtime-owned (Constitution §1.4).

loop_id.description: Threads charter→brief→checkpoint→spawn→trace→verdict→close (G6). The ledger file is named <loop_id>.jsonl.

seq.description: Monotonic per-loop sequence; first event seq=0, each subsequent +1 (verify_events enforces this).

ts.description: Event timestamp, injected by the caller (the pure hash path never reads the clock). Convention: ISO 8601. Stored verbatim as part of the hashed body.

type.description: Event type, e.g. loop_init | charter_loaded | checkpoint | spawn | verdict | close. Free-form string (the ledger is a generic event log).

payload.description: Event payload. Generic — the ledger stores whatever dict it is given. For a per-spawn event this is the execution-context record ($defs/spawn_payload, plan §4.5 G3).

prev_hash.description: sha256 hex (64 lowercase hex chars) of the previous event's hash; 64 zeros for the genesis event (GENESIS_PREV_HASH).

hash.description: sha256 hex over (prev_hash + canonical_json(event_without_hash)). Tampering any byte breaks the chain from this seq on.

spawn_payload.description: Per-spawn execution-context payload (plan §4.5 G3; audit_log.make_spawn_payload). Extends the orchestrator's calls/ record so a loop can be reconstructed with full harness/model/skill/memory/cost context.

prompt_ref.description: Run-dir-relative path to the as-dispatched prompt transcript (.orchestrator/audit/transcripts/<loop_id>/NNNN__<role>__prompt.md); always written. delivery-loop §4.2.10.

output_ref.description: Run-dir-relative path to the captured model-output transcript (…__output.{md,json}); null when the adapter produced no output (transport error). delivery-loop §4.2.10.

prompt_bytes.description: WP-0 measurement (observation-only): byte length of the as-dispatched prompt. Nullable/back-compatible — absent on pre-WP-0 ledgers.

memory_bytes.description: WP-0 measurement (observation-only): byte length of the Loop-Memory lessons block injected for the role (the byte companion to memory_injected).

fix_round.description: WP-0 measurement (observation-only): the fix-round index at dispatch (the fix-round cost multiplier).

load_graph_hash.description: WP-7 (observation-only): 'sha256:<16hex>' content fingerprint of the role's cold-start governance/kernel load set (load_sizer.cold_start_load_graph_hash) — records which governance VERSION an otherwise prompt-only-input_hash spawn loaded. Nullable/back-compatible — absent on pre-WP-7 ledgers. AUDIT-ONLY, not the Acceptance §3.5b reuse hash.

suppressed_lesson_ids.description: WP-6 (lessons tiering): ids of Loop-Memory lessons the tier-aware budget suppressed at ingress (so suppression is never silent — companion to memory_injected = the injected ids). Nullable/back-compatible — absent/null when no lessons block was injected (e.g. Acceptance) or on pre-WP-6 ledgers.

lesson_selection.description: WP-6 (lessons tiering): the deterministic ingress-selection audit. None when no lessons block was injected. Records the tier of every candidate, every suppression with its reason, and the bytes/tokens before & after bounding.

selected.description: Ids actually injected (full or compact), in injection order.

suppressed.description: Every dropped lesson with its suppression reason + classified tier.

tiers.description: id -> tier for ALL candidates (selected + suppressed).

representations.description: id -> representation for selected entries (compact = a PROMOTED reference).

bytes_before.description: Bytes of the unbounded legacy block (all candidates, full).

bytes_after.description: Bytes of the bounded block actually injected.

skill_reads.description: Universal-skill-mounting §3/D2: the adapter-OBSERVED SKILL.md reads (realpath-matched against the spawn's effective skill set; suffix fallback recorded, never conflated). [] = a successfully parsed stream with zero matching reads; null = nothing to record (no skills, or unobservable). Nullable/back-compatible.

skill_consumption.description: Universal-skill-mounting §3/D2 consumption state — MANDATORY (non-null) whenever the spawn's effective skill set is non-empty. none_observed is emitted ONLY from a successfully parsed stream with zero matching SKILL.md reads (never from an error). Agent self-report is NEVER a source. Null on pre-feature ledgers / skill-less spawns.

skill_consumption_reason.description: MANDATORY (non-null) whenever skill_consumption is 'unobservable' — the honest machine-readable why (never a bare unobservable): the harness exposes no read events / a captured stream failed to parse / the adapter raised before returning an envelope.

spawn_attempt.description: Universal-skill-mounting §3/D2: the run_with_monitor attempt index that produced the terminal result (earlier stuck attempts' streams are discarded, never merged). Nullable/back-compatible.

telemetry_source.description: 'adapter' = a SpawnResult envelope; 'legacy_normalized' = a plain-dict Adapter.spawn return normalized at the driver boundary (the deprecation signal — paired with a WARN adapter_legacy_return event). Nullable/back-compatible.
